name: Operating Room (DRAFT — scope TBD)

source.author: PLACEHOLDER — not from director materials
source.derivedFrom: NONE. The director sent no OR material, and the OR is a special case: the patient is often anesthetized, so 'OR communication' may mean pre-op consent/reassurance or TEAM communication rather than a bedside conversation. SCOPE MUST BE CLARIFIED WITH THE DIRECTOR before this is built properly.

description: DRAFT / UNVALIDATED / SCOPE UNRESOLVED. Provisional pre-op patient-communication criteria only. Do not rely on this until the director defines what the OR scenario covers.

coaching.whyItMatters: A calm pre-op introduction reassures an anxious patient before surgery.
examplePhrases: I'm Dr. ___, part of the team for your procedure today.

coaching.whyItMatters: Surfacing fears before surgery allows them to be addressed.
examplePhrases: Do you have any questions or worries about the procedure?

coaching.whyItMatters: Acknowledging pre-op anxiety builds trust at a vulnerable moment.
examplePhrases: It's completely normal to feel nervous before surgery.

coaching.whyItMatters: Clear, jargon-free explanation supports informed consent.
examplePhrases: Let me walk you through what will happen, step by step.

coaching.whyItMatters: Confirming understanding is part of valid consent.
examplePhrases: What questions do you have before we go ahead?

criteria.prompt: Did the clinician introduce themselves and their role?
Did the clinician ask about the patient's questions or worries about the procedure?
Did the clinician acknowledge pre-operative anxiety?
Did the clinician explain the procedure in plain language to support consent?
criteria.whatGoodLooksLike: Jargon-free walkthrough of what will happen.

criteria.prompt: Did the clinician confirm the patient's understanding and invite questions?
criteria.whatGoodLooksLike: Teach-back / explicit invitation for questions before proceeding.
